title: arum italicum subsp. canariense
intro: Arum italicum subsp. canariense, also known as Canary Island arum, is a subspecies of the Italian arum plant, belonging to the Arum genus. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Arum italicum subsp. canariense include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
